zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 開場固定收益論點

6月25日收盤延續美債漲勢，但其結構由週三中段領漲的牛市趨平，轉為溫和的牛市趨陡。2 年期殖利率收於 4.09%，10 年期收於 4.40%，政策敏感期限保留的單日漲幅大於長債。10 年期實質殖利率下降 4 個基點，同時損益兩平通膨率上升 3 個基點。結果呈現分歧的固定收益訊號：通膨沒有原先擔憂般嚴重，使投資人降低對實質利率的補償要求，但油價反轉上漲後，市場也恢復部分能源風險補償。

美國國內公布的一系列數據支持壓力緩解，卻不足以證實政策明確轉鴿。整體 PCE 通膨低於預期，核心通膨符合預期，促使市場小幅下調 Fed 緊縮預期。經濟活動細項具韌性，則指向相反方向。初領失業救濟金人數下降、第 1 季經濟成長上修、扣除運輸的耐久財需求增加，某地區製造業調查亦顯示經濟活動加速，價格指數更升至 2022 年以來最高。因此，即使前端上漲，Fed 官員仍維持限制性訊息。

能源改變了盤中行情的形態。荷莫茲海峽交通恢復之際，原油最初交易於接近戰前水準，但一艘貨輪遭到攻擊且數艘船隻折返後，油價隨即攀升。名目美債殖利率自低點反彈，長債收平，損益兩平通膨率則上升。因此，油價不再像 6月24日那樣是明確的通膨降溫助力，儘管其單週及單月仍大幅下跌，限制了通膨重新定價的幅度。

2股反向力量使本波漲勢維持有序且帶有條件。市場反應良好的 7 年期美債標售及穩健的國庫券投標，顯示政府供給可順利消化，未造成廣泛的融資中斷。海外方面，日本 20 年期標售需求疲弱、多年期投資計畫規模龐大，加上 BOJ 仍偏鷹，提高了全球長存續期間資產的出清利率。整體信用融資管道也維持暢通，但 SpaceX 債券在巨額首發後走弱，新的衍生性商品市場則反映可觀的保障成本。收盤行情延續存續期間壓力緩解，同時重新帶回能源、海外供給及發行人篩選風險。

## 美債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美債漲幅隨到期年限增加而縮小。2 年期與 5 年期殖利率各下降 2 個基點，至 4.09% 與 4.15%；10 年期下降 1 個基點至 4.40%，30 年期則維持在 4.86%。前端行情延續自週一 FOMC 後高點的大幅反轉，但以較長期間比較，存續期間仍占優勢：過去 1 個月，2 年期殖利率下降 4 個基點、5 年期下降 12 個基點、10 年期下降 16 個基點、30 年期下降 21 個基點。週四延續了這波較廣泛的漲勢，但各期限漲幅並不一致。

殖利率曲線斜率反映領漲部位轉移。2s10s 利差擴大 1 個基點至 31，2s30s 擴大 2 個基點至 77。由於 5 年期相對兩翼維持偏貴，2s5s10s 蝶式利差下降 1 個基點至-19，2s10s30s 蝶式利差則維持-15 不變。週三漲勢集中在中段與長端；週四新增漲幅轉向政策敏感期限。因此，長期財政與通膨補償仍比近期政策預期路徑更具抗跌性。

抗通膨債券市場提供最清楚的行情拆解。5 年期實質殖利率下降 6 個基點至 1.92%，7 年期下降 4 個基點至 2.05%，10 年期下降 4 個基點至 2.19%。20 年期及 30 年期實質殖利率各下降 2 個基點，至 2.51% 與 2.69%。10 年期名目殖利率僅下降 1 個基點，因損益兩平通膨率上升 3 個基點至 2.21%。投資人降低了整條殖利率曲線上的實質補償要求，但能源尾端風險再起，使名目長端未能跟上。

5月 PCE 通膨啟動了實質殖利率行情。整體指數月增 0.4%，低於 0.5%的預估中位數；核心價格則如預期上升 0.3%。整體年通膨率加速至 4.1%，核心通膨率升至 3.4%，後者為 2023 年以來最高，因此數據僅消除了上行意外，並未證明價格壓力已正常化。交換市場將截至 12月的預期升息幅度，由週三收盤約 36 個基點下調至約 34 個基點，7月升息的隱含機率則降至約 1/3。

其餘數據使 Fed 緊迫性下降的幅度保持有限。初領失業救濟金人數由修正後的 227,000 降至 215,000，但持續領取失業救濟金人數由 180萬增至 182.1萬。5月耐久財訂單繼 4月增加 8.5% 後下降 4.5%，主要因運輸設備下滑 14.0%；扣除運輸的訂單則增加 1.3%。第 1 季經濟成長上修至年化 2.1%。堪薩斯城聯準銀行綜合指數也由 8 升至 11，生產、訂單及就業均改善，2項主要價格指數皆升至 2022 年以來最高。

Fed 溝通符合此一成長與通膨訊號混雜的格局。芝加哥聯準銀行總裁 Austan Goolsbee 認為通膨報告的部分內容令人鼓舞，但強調核心通膨仍過高，且方向正在惡化。紐約聯準銀行總裁 John Williams 表示通膨無疑仍處高檔，並稱目前政策立場適合讓通膨重返目標。前一週會議後，19 名決策官員中有 9 名預測今年至少升息 1 次。市場擔憂的通膨超標並未出現，使這些訊息容許前端溫和上漲，但仍維持偏高的實質利率底部。

盤中交易顯示能源管道可多快抵銷數據的推動。PCE 公布後，各期限殖利率均觸及至少 1 週低點，30 年期更降至 4.82%，為 3月以來最低。荷莫茲事件後原油反轉，隨即帶動名目殖利率脫離低點。盤末僅較短期限仍明顯偏貴。因此，收盤行情將預期政策緊迫性下降，與能源及航運中斷可能拖慢整體通膨改善的疑慮區隔開來。

票息債供給強化了利率行情有序的一面。美國財政部的 $440億 7 年期標售以 4.260% 得標，與下午1 ET 截止前交易顯示的水準相符。標售公告後，指示殖利率曾高達 4.40%，其後需求被形容為良好。結果未產生新的新券讓利訊號，卻顯示即使殖利率已連續 2 天下降，中段仍能消化 7月7日的 3 年期標售前最後1筆固定利率債券供給。

短期國庫券標售也順利消化大量供給。$700億的 28 天期標售最高得標利率為 3.610%，投標倍數 2.74；間接投標人占獲配競爭性投標的 65.14%，交易商占 31.23%，直接投標人占 3.63%。$750億的 56 天期標售最高得標利率為 3.660%，投標倍數 2.79，間接投標占 69.52%，交易商占 27.85%，直接投標占 2.62%。2檔證券均於 6月30日發行並交割，使週四的投標結果與稍後的現金移轉分開。

現券殖利率在這些標售期限附近有所分化。4 週期票息等值殖利率上升 6 個基點至 3.68%，8 週期上升 2 個基點至 3.74%，6 週期則維持 3.69% 不變。13 週期與 17 週期殖利率各下降 1 個基點，26 週期則維持 3.94%。標售期限附近的局部壓力未擴散成共同的前端融資走勢。結合 7 年期標售結果，利率市場收盤格局仍是由能源通膨、需求具韌性及 Fed 維持限制性所約束的實質殖利率行情。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

廣泛掛牌信用資產報酬率小幅為正。LQD 上漲 0.0823%，HYG 上漲 0.0376%，JNK 上漲 0.0936%。由於美債漲勢在收盤前消退，投資等級基金僅獲得小幅存續期間助益，2檔投機等級基金則接近持平。整體市場穩定，既非廣泛追逐信用風險，也不是融資撤退。個別發行人的定價所含資訊，遠多於掛牌基金的溫和波動。

SpaceX 提供了最重要的當前現券信用更新。週二首次投資等級發行募得 $250億後，其債券相對美債走弱。10 年期債券週四以 1.57 個百分點的利差交易，高於發行時的 1.40 個百分點。利差擴大改變了對已完成交易的解讀：初級市場的強大承接能力依然明確，但債券進入正常交易、峰值認購簿不再支撐交易後，次級市場買方要求更高補償。

新的衍生性商品市場使這類區別更容易表達。交易商開始積極為 5 年期 SpaceX 信用違約交換造市，其中1項報價顯示年度中間價接近 1.255 個百分點，相當於每 $1,000萬的保障每年約需 $125,500。評等相近的 Intel 債務，其可比保障成本約為 0.64 個百分點。這項比較並未否定 SpaceX的投資等級評等或龐大流動性部位；它顯示投資人針對其資本密集度、治理權集中及距離產生自由現金流尚遠，要求獨特的溢價。

日本地區銀行展現了較高政策利率下另一種融資傳導。Yokohama Financial Group 第1次發行 ¥120億的 3 年期優先債，定價為 JGB 加 27 個基點。最終訂單約為發行額的 1.2 倍，買方包括壽險公司、投資顧問及信託銀行。BOJ 將政策利率調升至 1%，加劇存款競爭，促使貸款需求持續的地區銀行以批發融資補充存款。市場管道仍然存在，但認購簿僅屬溫和，使定價及未來發行容易受到當地融資需求影響。

中國境內市場的信用區別也日益明確。監管機關要求評等機構檢視最高級評等過度集中的情況，並將相對可比政府債券的發行利差納入評估指標。若發行人利差超過 200 個基點，可能在其他因素影響下失去 AAA 評等。截至第 1 季末，超過 6,000 家發行人中有 27% 獲評 AAA，另有 32% 為 AA+。較不集中的評等尺度可改善風險區隔，並讓較高借款成本集中於過去受惠於境內最高評等的發行人。

即使廣泛信用市場平靜，Strategy 特別股仍顯示傳統債券以外的融資如何惡化。Bitcoin 交易低於 $60,000 且買方退場之際，STRC 已由 $100的發行價跌至約 $75。此永續證券對 Strategy的 Bitcoin 持有部位沒有求償權、沒有到期日，董事會亦可降低或暫停股息；公司評等為 B-。其殖利率上升反映1項旨在支持持續累積 Bitcoin的融資工具正承受壓力，也凸顯資本結構較低層級所獲保障較弱。

綜合訊息是融資管道開放，但定價後的後果更加清楚。美債供給順利消化、掛牌信用資產報酬穩定，日本1家地區銀行也觸及新的投資人群體。然而，SpaceX的現券利差及 CDS 市場顯示，即使巨額發行成功，投資人仍需管理可觀的剩餘風險；Strategy 特別股則暴露了依賴波動性標的資產之融資的脆弱性。整體發行量維持強勁；次級市場定價、結構及債權人受償順位決定壓力出現的位置。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

日本構成最強的海外長存續期間反向力量。20 年期 JGB 標售投標倍數降至 2.97，為 5月 2025 以來最低，也低於 12 個月平均值。標售尾差為 1月以來最大，最低得標價遠低於標售前估值。由於保險公司及全球資產管理機構降低超長期 JGB 部位，銀行似乎承接了多數需求。正當美國實質殖利率下降時，疲弱的結果提高了殖利率曲線再次趨陡的可能性。

財政政策放大了標售訊號。首相 Sanae Takaichi的策略尋求在 14 年內，於人工智慧及半導體等領域吸引超過 ¥370兆、即 $2.3兆的公私部門投資。政府預測假設每年財政支出約 ¥10兆，並考慮發行過渡性債券，融資組合亦包含稅收及節支。成長效益將逐步浮現，但發債及債務比率暫時上升可能更早出現。這樣的時序要求長期 JGB 提供更多期限溢價。

貨幣政策未立即為買方基礎帶來緩解。BOJ 審議委員 Naoki Tamura 表示，在央行前一週將政策利率調升至 1% 後，每隔數月升息25個基點、朝接近 2%的中性利率前進，是他的基準情境。總裁 Kazuo Ueda 重申，後續行動取決於經濟與通膨。日圓持續疲弱增加輸入性價格壓力。日本政策利率及主權債殖利率上升，提高當地機構比較 JGB 與匯率避險後美債時所設定的門檻，有助解釋美國長債為何抗拒週四的實質殖利率行情。

由於能源價格已回吐戰時漲幅的大部分，歐洲的存續期間訊號較為溫和。Oxford Economics 在 6月升息後，取消了 ECB 再升息1次的預測，其他預測機構也減少預期升息次數。市場仍定價今年升息25個基點，而 ECB 執行理事 Isabel Schnabel 及管理委員會委員 Emmanuel Moulin 仍對通膨保持警覺。Moulin 將近期 25 個基點的升息形容為溫和，並指出油價已跌破最新預測所採用的假設。政策辯論轉向可能偏鷹的暫停，而非重新寬鬆。

英國公債顯示能源壓力緩解帶來的存續期間效益。英國 10 年期殖利率已降至約 4.7%，為 3月中以來最低；即使國內政治仍不確定，整體英國公債報酬仍創 3 個月新高。原油下跌減輕輸入性通膨負擔，使全球債市方向得以主導。週四盤末油價反彈削弱了這項支撐，但未將其消除；歐洲存續期間環境仍優於供給沉重的日本長端，卻也繼續暴露於新一波能源價格傳導。

中國則透過貨幣市場設計朝相反方向移動。中國人民銀行在自 5月 2025 以來維持 1.4% 不變的 7 天期基準工具之外，引入隔夜逆回購工具。新工具讓官員能更直接引導隔夜融資，並降低貨幣市場利率波動。鑑於第 2 季動能轉弱，交易員將這項框架變動與寬鬆傾向連結。更精準的短期流動性控制支持中國境內存續期間市場，與日本政策正常化及財政供給所造成的較高出清利率形成對比。

20年期國債標售直接顯示買方已要求更多保護。投標倍數降至3.0以下，需求為
2025年5月以來最弱，且明顯低於12個月平均值。日本保險公司5月出售國內
超長天期債券，全球資產管理機構也開始降低長天期JGB曝險。政府14年投資
計畫的公共融資與過渡債券需求仍不明朗，因此疲弱的標售參與已把潛在財政
疑慮轉化為可以觀察的市場承接限制。

東京通膨隨後提供較晚的亞洲時段更新。數據於紐約收盤後的 6月26日，上午8:30 JST 公布，首都扣除生鮮食品的 CPI 年增 1.6%，扣除生鮮食品及能源的指標則上升 1.9%。主要指標在 8 個月來首次加速，主因補貼結束後自來水服務費上漲。由於公布時間較晚，這將成為下一個 JGB 交易時段的輸入；週四美國長端的抗拒性已透過標售疲弱、財政計畫及日圓相關通膨風險顯現。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

儘管美元當日走軟，廣泛新興市場債務幾乎沒有變動。EMB 下跌 0.0104%，美元指數下跌 0.1771% 至 101.43。該美元指標過去 1 週仍上漲 0.5751%，過去 1 個月則上漲 2.1244%，使外部環境維持限制性。整體表現穩定，掩蓋了商品出口國、高評等主權發行人及正協商債務減免國家之間的大幅重新分配。

即使週四油價反彈，非洲石油出口國仍暴露於此前原油廣泛回落的影響。剛果共和國歐元債在 6月已下跌超過 2%，安哥拉、喀麥隆、加彭及奈及利亞也下跌超過 1%，相較之下新興市場平均上漲 0.5%。安哥拉 2048 年到期美元債殖利率由 6月初低點上升逾 60 個基點至 9.94%；剛果 2036 年到期債殖利率攀升約 66 個基點至 10.01%，奈及利亞 2034 年到期債殖利率則上升 33 個基點至 7.33%。油價下跌移除了出口收入支撐，使市場重新聚焦財政執行與外部融資。

這一組國家內部的差異相當重要。中國需求疲弱，使部分安哥拉原油品級跌至 10 多年來最低價，並降低市場對剛果原油的購買意願。由於 Dangote 煉油廠供應國內需求，奈及利亞原油品級表現較佳，而先前的匯率市場改革也增強該國韌性。週四荷莫茲事件使原油重新獲得即時安全風險溢價，但 WTI 與 Brent 過去 1 個月仍分別下跌超過 25% 及 27%。因此，石油相關信用債獲得短期價格支撐，卻未恢復先前的貿易條件優勢。

中國在主權信用光譜的另一端展現硬通貨融資能力。財政部推介最高 €50億的 5 年期、8 年期及 12 年期歐元債，初步價格指引分別為中間交換利率加 15、22 及 33 個基點。這筆交易可能成為中國規模最大的歐元主權債發行，距離1筆 €40億發行吸引超過 €1,000億需求僅 7 個月。中國提早重返市場，旨在歐洲可能出現通膨壓力及 ECB 進一步緊縮前，鎖定有利融資條件；所述條件僅為推介指引，而非最終定價。

債務減免談判暴露了另一種外部融資風險。中國呼籲在 G20 Common Framework 下，採取法律及技術措施限制債券持有人採取激進訴訟。在衣索比亞，投資人未能達成同時獲債券持有人團體及由中國共同領導的官方債權人接受的條件後，已開始就 $10億違約債券提起訴訟。這項爭議威脅將官方部門減免轉化為持久私人債權人協議所需的協調，也使優先債權人地位、負擔分攤及強制執行成為回收價值的核心。

本幣主權債顯示，政策可信度可抵銷部分全球美元限制。匈牙利 10 年期基準殖利率降至 5.16%，與可比英國債務的差距不到 0.5 個百分點；3月時其相對英國公債溢價曾為 2.6 個百分點。其相對德國的利差縮小至 2.3 個百分點，約為 3 個月前的一半。儘管匈牙利的信用評等較低，但可取得超過 €160億先前遭凍結的 EU 援助，加上承諾在 2030 前符合加入歐元區標準，均支持趨同。

菲律賓本幣債顯示，急遽反彈未必能夠持續。6月15日美伊臨時協議後，10年期殖利率下降約50個基點，為新興亞洲最強反彈；此前在衝突升級至6月12日期間，殖利率曾上升157個基點。BSP已將政策利率提高至4.75%，並把2026年通膨預測上調至6.4%，高於2%-4%目標；其中1項預測預期再升息3次、每次25個基點，至5.50%。能源價格下跌支撐行情，但負實質利率壓力、披索敏感度與聖嬰現象供給風險，使其不能被視為政策已完成轉向。

墨西哥則呈現政策維持不變，而非建立在預期寬鬆上的債券漲勢。Banxico將政策利率維持在6.50%，並暗示在核心通膨仍棘手之際延長暫停時間。第1季GDP萎縮0.6%，2026年成長預測也由1.6%下調至1.1%；但理事會仍保留通膨上行風險，並關注依數據調整的美國政策。決策後披索維持穩定，顯示眼前信用度充足；若核心物價持續，即使投資疲弱與貿易不確定，央行仍可能被迫採取行動。

因此，新興市場僅在整體層面保持穩定。中國能以很低的初步指引推出創紀錄規模的歐元債供給，匈牙利受惠於制度趨同題材，非洲出口國則失去原先支撐其美元債的石油溢價。衣索比亞面臨無法單靠市場准入解決的法律協調問題。美元當日走弱減輕眼前壓力，但其單月漲幅及偏高的美國實質殖利率，使再融資條件仍對商品收入、政策執行及債權人結構高度敏感。

## 跨資產

股票表現顯示，美債漲勢並非源自廣泛的成長恐慌。S&P 500 幾乎持平，下跌 0.0099%；Nasdaq 100 上漲 0.7538%，半導體指數則上漲 3.5864%。Micron的展望重振市場對 AI 硬體的信心，但 Apple 在調高 Mac、iPad 及家用裝置價格後下跌 6.1%。VIX 上升 1.3956% 至 18.89。晶片股強勢領漲並伴隨波動率上升，反映的是集中式分化，而非全面逃離風險。

指數表面平靜掩蓋了異常集中的AI曝險。截至週三收盤，8檔AI基礎設施相關股票貢獻S&P 500今年7.5%漲幅的近3分之2；半導體指數即使週二下跌7.9%，2026年漲幅仍約93%。因此，Micron反彈能支撐整體市場，但也意味針對少數公司的政策、融資或社會反彈衝擊，可能不成比例地傳導至股票波動率、信用供給與實質折現率。

油價與損益兩平通膨率上升直接一致。船隻遇襲打斷荷莫茲交通先前的正常化後，WTI 上漲 2.2462% 至 $71.92，Brent 上漲 2.0613% 至 $75.26。反轉前，兩者均交易於接近戰前水準。其單週跌幅仍約為 6%，單月跌幅則超過 25%，因此單日行情恢復了地緣政治溢價，卻未重新建立完整的戰時通膨衝擊。這項時間輪廓解釋了損益兩平通膨率為何上升，而實質殖利率與短期美債殖利率仍然下降。

美元與黃金提供了溫和避險訊號。美元指數在單月強勁上漲後回落至 101.43，黃金則上漲 1.0074% 至 $4,030.50。金價過去 1 週仍下跌 4.5832%，過去 1 個月下跌 10.8494%。美元走軟及金價走強符合週四地緣政治疑慮再起，但兩者較長期走勢仍與實質利率偏高及 Fed 相對偏鷹一致。2個市場均未顯示美元流動性出現斷裂。

因此，跨資產表現使 6月24日的明確通膨降溫敘事變得複雜，但並未以衰退風險取而代之。半導體上漲、廣泛股票指數持平，信用基金也維持穩定，支持總體數據中需求具韌性的一面。油價與金價對荷莫茲不確定性再起作出反應，推升尾端通膨風險並限制長債。整體一致的解讀是受控的實質殖利率行情：政策緊迫性略微下降，但能源安全及集中的科技風險阻止名目存續期間出現更強漲勢。

## 前瞻市場意義

6月26日，上午10:00 ET，密西根大學最終消費者調查及紐約聯準銀行的 Multivariate Core Trend Inflation 指標，將是最接近的考驗，以判斷週四 PCE 帶來的緩解能否抵禦能源溢價再起。密西根的 6月初值為消費者信心 48.9、1 年期通膨預期 4.6%、長期通膨預期 3.4%。若最終通膨預期下降、趨勢通膨指標轉弱，尤其在原油仍接近事件後價格區間時，將支持實質殖利率下降，並證實 PCE 結果在不使較長期通膨定價失去錨定的情況下降低政策緊迫性。若預期持穩或上升，且核心趨勢壓力持續，則會挑戰此一分化格局，影響將透過損益兩平通膨率、2 年期至 10 年期實質殖利率及 12月 Fed 定價傳導，並重新帶回週四牛市趨陡可能只是市場躲過上行意外後短暫緩解的可能性。

日本買方基礎問題將透過 3 場主權債標售發展：6月26日約下午12:35 JST 標售約 ¥4.1兆的 3 個月期國庫券、6月30日標售約 ¥2.8兆的 2 年期 JGB，以及 7月2日標售約 ¥2.6兆的新 10 年期債券。若前端及基準期限均呈現廣泛需求且殖利率穩定，將表示疲弱的 20 年期標售僅集中於超長存續期間，從而消除部分財政擴張與 BOJ 正常化正推升整條國內出清殖利率曲線的疑慮。若需求反覆疲弱或殖利率曲線再次趨陡，尤其 6月30日的經濟活動數據及 7月1日 Tankan 顯示投資與價格預期穩健，則將證實日本期限及政策溢價上升。敏感的傳導路徑包括日圓、JGB 曲線斜率、日本對匯率避險後外國債券的需求，以及美國 30 年期殖利率。

美國季末現金需求在 7月2日提早收盤前面臨密集事件。美國財政部將於 6月29日標售 $920億的 13 週期及 $790億的 26 週期國庫券，並於 7月2日交割，隨後於 6月30日標售 $800億的 6 週期國庫券；6月非農就業及每週初領失業救濟金數據於 7月2日，上午8:30 ET 公布，4 週期及 8 週期配對標售接著於 11:30 舉行，固定收益交易預定於下午2:00 收盤。若投標倍數有序、最終買方廣泛參與且現券國庫券殖利率穩定，將證實週四標售反映可持續通過交割轉折點的流動性。較疲弱的勞動報告將把此一訊號延伸至 Fed 定價及曲線中段。若承接以交易商為主、國庫券突然轉便宜，或非農就業與薪資成長強勁，則會在縮短的交易時段中結合資產負債表壓力與緊縮風險再起，挑戰有序牛市趨陡的論點。

overall_summary.headline: 通膨較溫和壓低實質殖利率，推動美債上漲，但油價限制存續期間行情
overall_summary.session_narrative: 紐約收盤時，2 年期與 5 年期券保有溫和漲幅，長債則收平，形成小幅牛市趨陡。低於市場憂慮程度的 PCE 數據壓低實質殖利率，並略微降低市場對 Fed 緊縮的預期。油價反彈推升損益兩平通膨率，加上經濟活動穩健、Fed 指引偏鷹，以及日本對長存續期間債券需求疲弱，使名目長端未能跟上實質利率的漲勢。
overall_summary.summary: PCE 通膨未出現上行意外，由政策敏感期限領漲的美債行情得以延續至紐約收盤。殖利率下行主要由實質殖利率帶動，但油價走強、經濟活動具韌性及 Fed 維持限制性指引，限制了名目長端的漲幅。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤走勢有利於近期政策壓力緩解，但對長存續期間的信心仍受約束。
section_summaries.next_check: 必須觀察油價漲勢能否延續及下一批通膨訊號，以判斷緩解行情能否擴大。
section_summaries.summary: PCE 通膨未出現上行意外後，政策敏感型美債引領殖利率曲線溫和牛市趨陡，且本波漲勢主要由實質殖利率下降帶動。油價反彈恢復部分通膨補償，而經濟活動具韌性、Fed 指引維持限制性、海外供給壓力及部分信用債走弱，使行情仍帶有條件。

section_summaries.market_read: 本波漲勢反映政策緊迫性降低，而非限制性利率環境已經瓦解。

section_summaries.market_read: 整體信用穩定，掩蓋了結構、受償順位及發行人品質之間日益擴大的差距。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The June 25 close extended the Treasury rally, but its composition changed from Wednesday’s belly-led bull flattening to a modest bull steepening. The 2-year yield closed at 4.09% and the 10-year at 4.40%, with policy-sensitive maturities retaining more of the day’s gains than the long bond. A 4-basis-point decline in the 10-year real yield coexisted with a 3-basis-point rise in breakeven inflation. The result was a split fixed-income message: investors reduced the real-rate penalty after inflation proved less severe than feared, yet restored some compensation for energy risk as oil reversed higher.

The domestic release block supported relief without validating an outright dovish turn. Headline PCE inflation undershot expectations and core inflation matched them, prompting a small reduction in expected Fed tightening. Resilient activity details pointed the other way. New unemployment claims fell, Q1 growth was revised higher, durable-goods demand outside transportation advanced, and a regional factory survey combined faster activity with the strongest price indexes since 2022. Fed officials consequently retained a restrictive message even as the front end rallied.

Energy changed the intraday shape of the move. Crude initially traded near prewar levels as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz recovered, then climbed after a cargo ship was struck and several vessels turned back. Nominal Treasury yields rebounded from their lows, the long bond finished unchanged, and breakevens rose. Oil therefore ceased to be the clean disinflationary ally it had been on June 24, although its large weekly and monthly decline still limited the scale of the inflation repricing.

Two counterforces kept the rally orderly and conditional. A well-received 7-year Treasury auction and solid bill participation showed that government supply could clear without a broad funding disruption. Abroad, weak demand at Japan’s 20-year auction, an expansive multi-year investment program and a still-hawkish BOJ raised the global clearing rate for long duration. Credit access also remained open at the aggregate level, but SpaceX bonds weakened after their jumbo debut and a new derivatives market priced meaningful protection costs. The close continued duration relief while reopening energy, foreign-supply and issuer-selection risks.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury gains diminished with maturity. The 2- and 5-year yields each fell 2 basis points, to 4.09% and 4.15%, while the 10-year declined 1 basis point to 4.40% and the 30-year held at 4.86%. The front-end move followed a sharp reversal from Monday’s post-FOMC highs, yet the longer comparison still favored duration: over 1 month, yields were 4 basis points lower at 2 years, 12 lower at 5 years, 16 lower at 10 years and 21 lower at 30 years. Thursday preserved that broader rally without extending it evenly.

Curve slopes captured the shift in leadership. The 2s10s spread widened 1 basis point to 31, and 2s30s widened 2 to 77. The 2s5s10s butterfly fell 1 basis point to -19 as the 5-year stayed rich against its wings, while the 2s10s30s butterfly was unchanged at -15. Wednesday’s rally had concentrated in the belly and long end; Thursday transferred the incremental gain toward policy-sensitive maturities. Long-run fiscal and inflation compensation consequently remained more resistant than the expected path of near-term policy.

Inflation-linked markets supplied the clearest decomposition. The 5-year real yield fell 6 basis points to 1.92%, the 7-year dropped 4 to 2.05%, and the 10-year declined 4 to 2.19%. 20- and 30-year real yields each eased 2 basis points, to 2.51% and 2.69%. At 10 years, the nominal yield fell only 1 basis point because breakeven inflation rose 3 to 2.21%. Investors accepted less real compensation across the curve, but the renewed energy tail kept the nominal long end from matching that move.

May PCE inflation initiated the real-yield rally. The headline index rose 0.4% on the month, below the 0.5% median estimate, while core prices increased 0.3% as expected. Annual headline inflation accelerated to 4.1% and core inflation to 3.4%, the latter its highest since 2023, so the release offered relief from an upside surprise rather than evidence that price pressure had normalized. Swaps reduced tightening priced through December to about 34 basis points from roughly 36 at Wednesday’s close, and the implied chance of a July increase fell to about 1 in 3.

The rest of the data kept the reduction in Fed urgency narrow. Initial claims dropped to 215,000 from a revised 227,000, even as insured unemployment rose to 1.821 million from 1.800 million. May durable-goods orders fell 4.5% after an 8.5% April increase, driven by a 14.0% decline in transportation equipment; orders excluding transportation rose 1.3%. Q1 growth was revised to a 2.1% annualized pace. The Kansas City Fed composite also rose to 11 from 8, with production, orders and employment improving and both principal price indexes reaching their highest readings since 2022.

Fed communication fit that mixed growth-and-inflation configuration. Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee regarded parts of the inflation release as encouraging, but emphasized that core inflation remained too high and was moving in the wrong direction. New York Fed President John Williams called inflation unquestionably elevated and described the current policy stance as well positioned to return it to target. Nine of 19 policymakers had projected at least 1 increase this year after the prior week’s meeting. Those messages allowed a modest front-end rally after the feared inflation overshoot failed to appear, while preserving a high real-rate floor.

Intraday trading showed how quickly the energy channel could offset the data impulse. Yields across maturities reached their lowest levels in at least a week after the PCE release, and the 30-year touched 4.82%, its lowest since March. Crude’s reversal after the Hormuz incident then pulled nominal yields away from those lows. Only shorter maturities remained clearly richer late in the session. The close therefore separated lower expected policy urgency from renewed concern that energy and shipping disruption could slow progress on headline inflation.

Coupon supply reinforced the orderly side of the rates story. Treasury’s $44 billion 7-year auction was awarded at 4.260%, matching the level indicated in trading immediately before the 1 p.m. ET deadline. Demand was described as good after the indicated yield had traded as high as 4.40% following the auction announcement. The result did not create a new concession signal, but it showed that the belly could absorb the final fixed-rate sale before the July 7 3-year auction even after 2 days of falling yields.

Short-bill auctions also cleared in size. The $70 billion 28-day sale stopped at a 3.610% high rate with a 2.74 bid-to-cover ratio; indirect bidders took 65.14% of accepted competitive tenders, dealers 31.23% and direct bidders 3.63%. The $75 billion 56-day sale stopped at 3.660% with 2.79 coverage, a 69.52% indirect share, a 27.85% dealer share and a 2.62% direct share. Both securities issue and settle on June 30, keeping Thursday’s bidding outcome separate from the later cash transfer.

Cash yields were differentiated around those sales. The 4-week coupon-equivalent yield rose 6 basis points to 3.68% and the 8-week added 2 to 3.74%, while 6 weeks was unchanged at 3.69%. 13- and 17-week yields each fell 1 basis point, and the 26-week yield held at 3.94%. The local pressure around the auctioned maturities did not spread into a common front-end funding move. Taken with the 7-year result, the pattern leaves the rates close as a real-yield rally constrained by energy inflation, resilient demand and a still-restrictive Fed.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Broad listed credit returns were slightly positive. LQD gained 0.0823%, HYG rose 0.0376% and JNK added 0.0936%. The high-grade fund received only a small duration benefit because the Treasury rally faded into the close, while the 2 speculative-grade funds held near flat. This was a stable aggregate tape, neither a broad search for credit risk nor a withdrawal of financing. Issuer-level pricing carried much more information than the modest moves in the listed funds.

SpaceX supplied the most important current cash-credit update. After raising $25 billion in its debut high-grade sale on Tuesday, its bonds weakened relative to Treasuries. The 10-year notes traded at a spread of 1.57 percentage points on Thursday after pricing at 1.40 percentage points. The widening changed the interpretation of the completed deal: exceptional primary capacity remained evident, but secondary buyers required more compensation once the bonds entered normal trading and the peak orderbook was no longer supporting the transaction.

A new derivatives market made that discrimination easier to express. Dealers began actively making markets in 5-year SpaceX credit-default swaps, with 1 indication showing a midpoint near 1.255 percentage points annually, or about $125,500 per year for $10 million of protection. Comparable protection on similarly rated Intel debt was around 0.64 percentage point. The comparison does not negate SpaceX’s high-grade ratings or large liquidity position; it shows that investors assign a distinct premium to its capital intensity, governance concentration and long path toward free cash generation.

Japanese regional banks demonstrated a different funding transmission from higher policy rates. Yokohama Financial Group sold its inaugural ¥12 billion 3-year senior bond at 27 basis points over JGBs. Final orders reached about 1.2 times the issue, with life insurers, investment advisers and trust banks among the buyers. The BOJ’s increase to a 1% policy rate intensified competition for deposits, encouraging regional lenders with continuing loan demand to supplement deposits with wholesale funding. Access was available, though the modest book left pricing and future issuance sensitive to local funding needs.

Credit differentiation was also becoming more explicit in China’s domestic market. Regulators asked rating firms to review the concentration of top-tier grades and introduced issuance spreads over comparable government bonds as one assessment metric. An issuer whose spread exceeds 200 basis points could risk losing AAA status, subject to other factors. At the end of Q1, 27% of more than 6,000 issuers were rated AAA and another 32% AA+. A less compressed ratings scale could improve risk separation, with higher borrowing costs concentrated among issuers that previously benefited from the top domestic label.

Strategy’s preferred shares showed how funding can deteriorate outside conventional bonds even when broad credit remains calm. STRC had fallen to about $75 from its $100 issue price as Bitcoin traded below $60,000 and buyers retreated. The perpetual security has no claim on Strategy’s Bitcoin holdings, no maturity date, and dividends that the board may reduce or suspend; the company carries a B- rating. Its rising yield reflects strain in a funding vehicle designed to support continued Bitcoin accumulation and highlights the weaker protection available lower in the capital structure.

The combined message was open access with more visible post-pricing consequences. Treasury supply cleared, listed credit returns were steady and a regional Japanese bank reached a new investor base. SpaceX’s cash spread and CDS market nevertheless showed that a successful jumbo sale can leave investors managing meaningful residual risk, while Strategy’s preferred exposed the fragility of funding dependent on an underlying volatile asset. Aggregate volume remained strong; secondary pricing, structure and creditor seniority determined where the strain appeared.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

Japan presented the strongest foreign long-duration counterweight. The 20-year JGB auction’s bid-to-cover ratio fell to 2.97, its weakest since May 2025 and below the 12-month average. The tail was the widest since January, and the lowest accepted price fell well below the pre-sale estimate. Banks appeared to provide much of the demand as insurers and global managers reduced exposure to super-long JGBs. The weak result raised the prospect of renewed curve steepening just as U.S. real yields were falling.

Fiscal policy amplified the auction signal. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s strategy seeks more than ¥370 trillion, or $2.3 trillion, of public and private investment over 14 years in areas including artificial intelligence and semiconductors. Government projections assume annual fiscal spending around ¥10 trillion and contemplate bridging bonds, with tax revenue and spending savings also part of the funding mix. Growth benefits would arrive gradually, whereas issuance and a temporary increase in the debt ratio could appear earlier. That sequencing demands more term premium from long JGBs.

Monetary policy offered little immediate relief to the buyer base. BOJ board member Naoki Tamura described quarter-point increases every few months toward a neutral rate near 2% as his baseline, after the bank raised the policy rate to 1% the prior week. Governor Kazuo Ueda reiterated that further moves depend on the economy and inflation. Persistent yen weakness adds imported-price pressure. Higher local policy and sovereign yields increase the hurdle Japanese institutions apply when comparing JGBs with currency-hedged Treasuries, helping explain the U.S. long bond’s resistance to Thursday’s real-yield rally.

Europe’s duration signal was softer because energy had already retraced much of its wartime rise. Oxford Economics dropped its forecast for another ECB increase after the June move, and other forecasters reduced their expected number of steps. Markets still priced 25 bp increase this year, while ECB Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel and Governing Council member Emmanuel Moulin retained inflation vigilance. Moulin described the recent 25-basis-point increase as moderate and noted that oil had fallen below the assumptions used in the latest forecasts. The policy debate shifted toward a possible hawkish pause, not renewed accommodation.

Gilts illustrated the duration benefit from that energy relief. The 10-year UK yield had fallen to around 4.7%, its lowest since mid-March, and broad gilt returns reached a 3-month high even through domestic political uncertainty. Lower crude reduced the imported-inflation burden and allowed global bond direction to dominate. Thursday’s late oil rebound qualified this support without erasing it, leaving European duration more favorable than Japan’s supply-heavy long end but still exposed to another round of energy pass-through.

China moved in the opposite direction through money-market design. The PBOC introduced an overnight reverse-repo instrument alongside its 1.4% 7-day benchmark, which has been unchanged since May 2025. The new tool gives officials a more direct way to steer overnight funding and narrow volatility in money-market rates. Traders associated the framework change with an easing bias amid weaker Q2 momentum. More precise short-term liquidity control supports China’s domestic duration market, contrasting with the higher clearing rate created by Japan’s policy normalization and fiscal supply.

The 20-year auction supplied direct evidence that buyers were already demanding
more protection. Its bid-to-cover ratio slipped below 3.0, the weakest demand
since May 2025 and well below the 12-month average. The result followed selling
of super-long domestic bonds by Japanese insurers in May and emerging cuts to
long-duration JGB exposure among global managers. With the government's
14-year investment plan still unclear on public financing and bridge-bond
needs, weak auction participation turned a prospective fiscal concern into an
observable absorption constraint.

Tokyo inflation then supplied a later Asia-window update. Released at 8:30 a.m. JST on June 26, after the New York close, the capital’s CPI excluding fresh food rose 1.6% from a year earlier and the measure excluding fresh food and energy increased 1.9%. The key gauge accelerated for the first time in 8 months, led by water-service fees after subsidies ended. Its timing makes it an input for the next JGB session; Thursday’s U.S. long-end resistance was already visible through the weak auction, fiscal plan and yen-linked inflation risk.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Broad emerging-market debt was almost unchanged despite a softer dollar on the day. EMB slipped 0.0104%, while the Dollar Index fell 0.1771% to 101.43. The currency gauge was still 0.5751% higher over 1 week and 2.1244% higher over 1 month, preserving a restrictive external backdrop. Stable aggregate performance concealed a large redistribution between commodity exporters, highly rated sovereign issuers and countries negotiating debt relief.

African oil exporters remained exposed to the broader retreat in crude even after Thursday’s rebound. Republic of Congo eurobonds had lost more than 2% during June, and Angola, Cameroon, Gabon and Nigeria more than 1%, against an average 0.5% gain across emerging markets. Angola’s 2048 dollar-bond yield rose more than 60 basis points from its early-June low to 9.94%; Congo’s 2036 yield climbed about 66 basis points to 10.01%, and Nigeria’s 2034 yield rose 33 basis points to 7.33%. Cheaper oil removed export-income support and forced renewed attention to fiscal execution and external funding.

The country differences within that group were material. Weak Chinese demand pushed some Angolan crude grades to their lowest prices in more than a decade and reduced buying interest in Congolese oil. Nigerian grades held up better because the Dangote refinery supplied domestic demand, while prior currency-market reforms improved the country’s resilience. Thursday’s Hormuz incident restored an immediate security premium to crude, but WTI and Brent remained down more than 25% and 27% over 1 month. Oil credits therefore gained short-run price support without recovering the earlier terms-of-trade advantage.

China demonstrated hard-currency access at the other end of the sovereign spectrum. The Ministry of Finance marketed as much as €5 billion of 5-, 8- and 12-year euro bonds at initial guidance of 15, 22 and 33 basis points over mid-swaps. The transaction could become China’s largest euro sovereign sale and came only 7 months after a €4 billion issue drew more than €100 billion of demand. The early return to the market sought to capture favorable funding before possible European inflation pressure and further ECB tightening; the stated terms established marketing, not completed pricing.

Debt-relief negotiations exposed a different external-financing risk. China called for legal and technical measures to limit aggressive bondholder litigation under the Group of 20 Common Framework. In Ethiopia, investors had begun the process of suing over $1 billion of defaulted notes after failing to reach terms acceptable to both the bondholder group and official creditors co-led by China. The dispute threatens the coordination needed to translate official-sector relief into a durable private-creditor settlement and leaves preferred-creditor status, burden sharing and enforcement central to recovery values.

Local-currency sovereigns showed that policy credibility can offset part of the global dollar constraint. Hungary’s 10-year benchmark yield fell to 5.16%, within half a percentage point of comparable UK debt, after the premium over gilts had stood at 2.6 percentage points in March. Its spread over Germany narrowed to 2.3 percentage points, about half the level 3 months earlier. Access to more than €16 billion of previously frozen EU aid and a pledge to meet euro-entry criteria by 2030 supported convergence despite Hungary’s lower credit rating.

Philippine local bonds illustrated why a sharp rebound need not be durable. 10-year yields fell about 50 basis points after the June 15 U.S.-Iran interim agreement, the strongest recovery in emerging Asia, after rising 157 basis points during the conflict escalation through June 12. The BSP had already raised its policy rate to 4.75% and lifted its 2026 inflation forecast to 6.4%, above the 2%-4% target; one forecast called for 3 more quarter-point increases to 5.50%. Cheaper energy supported the rally, but negative real-rate pressure, peso sensitivity and El Niño supply risk argued against treating it as a completed policy turn.

Mexico supplied a contrasting hold rather than a rally built on prospective easing. Banxico kept its policy rate at 6.50% and signaled a longer pause as core inflation remained difficult despite softer headline prices. GDP had contracted 0.6% in Q1, and the 2026 growth forecast had been cut to 1.1% from 1.6%, but the board retained upside inflation risks and sensitivity to data-dependent U.S. policy. A stable peso after the decision showed immediate credibility; persistence in core prices would still force action despite weak investment and trade uncertainty.

The EM tape was consequently stable only in aggregate. China could bring record-scale euro supply at tight initial guidance, Hungary benefited from an institutional-convergence story, and African exporters lost the oil premium that had supported their dollar bonds. Ethiopia faced a legal coordination problem that market access alone could not solve. Dollar weakness on the day eased immediate pressure, yet the currency’s monthly strength and high U.S. real yields kept refinancing conditions sensitive to commodity revenue, policy execution and creditor structure.

## Cross asset

Equity performance argued against a broad growth scare as the source of the Treasury rally. The S&P 500 was virtually unchanged, down 0.0099%, while the Nasdaq 100 gained 0.7538% and the semiconductor index rose 3.5864%. Micron’s outlook revived confidence in AI hardware, although Apple fell 6.1% after raising prices across Macs, iPads and home devices. The VIX increased 1.3956% to 18.89. Strong chip leadership alongside higher volatility described concentrated dispersion, not a uniform flight from risk.

The index-level calm masked unusually concentrated AI exposure. 8 infrastructure-linked stocks had generated almost 2-thirds of the S&P 500’s 7.5% year-to-date gain through Wednesday, while the semiconductor index remained about 93% higher for 2026 even after its 7.9% Tuesday drop. That concentration made Micron’s recovery supportive for the broad tape, but it also meant policy, financing or public-backlash shocks around a small group could transmit disproportionately through equity volatility, credit supply and real discount rates.

Oil aligned directly with the rise in breakeven inflation. WTI gained 2.2462% to $71.92 and Brent rose 2.0613% to $75.26 after the vessel strike disrupted the earlier normalization in Hormuz traffic. Both had traded near prewar levels before the reversal. Their 1-week losses remained about 6%, and their 1-month declines exceeded 25%, so the daily move restored a geopolitical premium without re-establishing the full wartime inflation shock. That time-profile explains why breakevens rose while real yields and short Treasury yields still declined.

The dollar and gold offered a mild hedge signal. The Dollar Index eased to 101.43 after a strong monthly advance, and gold gained 1.0074% to $4,030.50. Bullion was still 4.5832% lower over 1 week and 10.8494% lower over 1 month. A softer dollar and firmer gold matched renewed geopolitical concern on Thursday, while their longer moves remained consistent with elevated real rates and a relatively hawkish Fed. Neither market signaled a break in dollar liquidity.

Cross assets therefore complicated the clean disinflation narrative from June 24 without replacing it with recession risk. Semiconductors rallied, the broad equity index held flat and credit funds were stable, supporting the resilient-demand side of the macro releases. Oil and gold reacted to renewed Hormuz uncertainty, lifting the inflation tail and restraining the long bond. The coherent interpretation is a controlled real-yield rally in which policy urgency eased slightly, while energy security and concentrated technology risk prevented a stronger nominal-duration move.

## Forward Market Significance

At 10:00 a.m. ET on June 26, the final University of Michigan consumer survey and the New York Fed’s Multivariate Core Trend Inflation measure provide the nearest test of whether Thursday’s PCE relief can survive a renewed energy premium. Michigan’s preliminary June readings were 48.9 for sentiment, 4.6% for 1-year inflation expectations and 3.4% for long-run expectations. Lower final expectations and a softer trend-inflation measure, especially if crude remains near its post-incident range, would support the decline in real yields and confirm that the PCE result reduced policy urgency without unanchoring longer inflation pricing. Firm or rising expectations alongside persistent core-trend pressure would challenge that split, transmitting through breakevens, 2- to 10-year real yields and December Fed pricing, and would reopen the possibility that Thursday’s bull steepening was only temporary relief from a feared upside surprise.

Japan’s buyer-base question moves through 3 sovereign auctions: about ¥4.1 trillion of 3-month bills around 12:35 p.m. JST on June 26, roughly ¥2.8 trillion of 2-year JGBs on June 30 and about ¥2.6 trillion of new 10-year bonds on July 2. Broad demand and stable yields across the front end and benchmark maturity would indicate that the weak 20-year sale was concentrated in super-long duration, resolving part of the concern that fiscal expansion and BOJ normalization are lifting the entire domestic clearing curve. Repeatedly soft demand or renewed steepening, particularly if June 30 activity releases and the July 1 Tankan show firm investment and price expectations, would confirm a higher Japanese term and policy premium. The sensitive transmission runs through the yen, JGB slopes, Japanese demand for currency-hedged foreign bonds and the U.S. 30-year yield.

U.S. quarter-end cash demand faces a dense sequence before the July 2 early close. Treasury sells $92 billion of 13-week and $79 billion of 26-week bills on June 29 for July 2 settlement, followed by an $80 billion 6-week sale on June 30; June payrolls and weekly claims arrive at 8:30 a.m. ET on July 2, paired 4- and 8-week auctions follow at 11:30, and fixed-income trading is scheduled to close at 2:00 p.m. Orderly coverage, broad end-buyer participation and stable cash-bill yields would confirm that Thursday’s auctions reflected durable liquidity through the settlement turn. A cooler labor report would extend that signal into Fed pricing and the belly. Dealer-heavy absorption, abrupt bill cheapening or strong payroll and wage growth would challenge the orderly bull-steepening thesis by combining balance-sheet pressure with renewed tightening risk in a shortened session.

section_summaries.market_read: The close favors near-term policy relief but leaves long-duration conviction constrained.

section_summaries.summary: Policy-sensitive Treasuries led a modest bull steepening after PCE inflation avoided an upside surprise, while falling real yields carried most of the rally. Oil’s rebound restored some inflation compensation, and resilient activity, restrictive Fed guidance, foreign supply pressure and selective credit weakness kept the move conditional.

Real yields declined more than nominals, breakevens rose, and solid coupon and bill auctions showed supply cleared without a broad funding strain.

section_summaries.market_read: Stable aggregate credit masks widening gaps in structure, seniority and issuer quality.
section_summaries.next_check: Post-deal SpaceX pricing and lower-tier funding stress remain the clearest tests.
section_summaries.summary: Aggregate credit remained steady and funding access stayed open, yet secondary markets demanded sharper issuer discrimination. SpaceX weakened after its jumbo debut, Japanese regional-bank access came with a modest book, and Strategy’s preferred shares exposed structural fragility where volatile collateral and weak creditor protections underpin financing.

section_summaries.market_read: Japanese term premium is the main foreign constraint on the U.S. long-duration rally.
section_summaries.next_check: Upcoming JGB auctions must separate super-long weakness from a broader buyer retreat.

section_summaries.summary: Japan’s weak long-bond auction, fiscal investment plan and ongoing BOJ normalization raised the clearing rate for global duration, limiting U.S. long-end performance. Europe benefited from energy’s broader retracement, while China’s new overnight liquidity tool pointed toward more precise easing and a contrasting domestic duration backdrop.

section_summaries.market_read: Dollar relief eased pressure, but Philippine inflation and Mexico’s core-price persistence constrained local duration.

African oil exporters, China, Hungary and Ethiopia retained sharply different funding paths.

section_summaries.market_read: Risk resilience validates lower real yields, while concentrated AI leadership and oil keep both tails alive.
section_summaries.next_check: The durability of leadership across 8 AI-linked stocks and the Hormuz premium will test that balance.
section_summaries.summary: Cross assets rejected a broad recession interpretation, but 8 AI infrastructure stocks had generated almost 2-thirds of the S&P 500’s 7.5% yearly gain, leaving index resilience unusually concentrated. Oil and gold reflected renewed geopolitical concern, while stable credit and a modestly softer dollar argued against a liquidity break.

section_summaries.market_read: The rally survives only if inflation, global duration and cash demand remain orderly.
section_summaries.next_check: The June 26-to-July 2 sequence will reveal which constraint becomes dominant.
section_summaries.summary: 3 tests determine whether the conditional rally can persist: near-term U.S. inflation expectations, Japan’s auction demand across maturities, and quarter-end U.S. bill absorption alongside labor data. Benign outcomes would extend real-yield relief; firmer inflation, weak Japanese demand or funding pressure would restore term and policy premia.
